item.title: Australia's Collins Foods to transfer 20 Taco Bell outlets to Yum Brands
item.description: Australia's Collins Foods said вҖӢon Tuesday it would hand over 20 of its 27 вҖҢTaco Bell restaurants in the country to an affiliate of Yum Brands and Restaurant Brands, moving closer to a full exit from the loss-making business.

item.title: Oil rises for a fourth day on supply cuts from widening Middle East conflict
item.description: Iran's effective closure of the Strait of вҒ Hormuz has вҖӢpushed Brent futures up 59% so far in March, its highest monthly gain ever, while WTI is up 58% this month, вҖӢthe most since May 2020.

item.title: American Exchange Group to buy sneaker maker Allbirds for $39 million
item.description: American Exchange Group (AXNY) entered an agreement on Monday to buy all вҖӢthe assets and liabilities of вҖҢfootwear maker Allbirds for $39 million.

item.title: Australian $240 billion pension fund snaps up Japanese, European stocks and UK bonds
item.description: Australia's No.2 pension fund has вҖӢincreased its global equities, Australian and British bond investments, some of the world's most sold-off вҖҢasset classes, in the past month to take advantage of financial market volatility created by the Iran war.

item.title: China establishes data organisation aimed at global industry consensus
item.description: China has launched the World Data Organisation, a professional body вҖӢto promote orderly data development and вҖҢutilisation as well as its governance globally, according to state television CCTV.

item.title: Samsung SDI to lend $1.05 billion to Stellantis JV StarPlus Energy
item.description: South Korea's вҖӢSamsung SDI вҖҢsaid it plans to вҖӢlend вҖӢ1.6 trillion won ($1.05 вҒ billion) to вҖӢStarPlus Energy, вҖӢits battery joint venture with вҖӢStellantis.

item.title: US pump prices hit $4 a gallon as Iran war wreaks havoc on global energy supply
item.description: The U.S. national average retail вҖӢprice of gasoline crossed $4 a gallon for the first time in more than three years on Monday, data вҖҢfrom price tracking services GasBuddy showed, as the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran continued to roil global energy markets.

item.title: War sets dollar for monthly rise, yen recovers on intervention threat
item.description: The dollar headed for its biggest monthly gain since July on Tuesday and stands out as the strongest so-called safe asset as war вҖӢin the Mideast has set oil prices surging and raised the risk of global recession.

item.title: Australia central bank uncertain on path for rates after March hike
item.description: SYDNEY, March 31 (Reuters) - Australia's central bank board felt monetary policy needed to be restrictive when it decided to raise rates earlier this month in a split decision, although it was вҖӢready to respond should the conflict in the Middle East lead to an вҖҢeconomic downturn.

item.title: American Express named official payments partner of NFL
item.description: American Express has signed вҖӢa multi-year deal to become the National Football League's official payments partner starting вҖҢwith the 2026 season, the company and the league said on Monday.

item.title: Australia investigates tech giants over social media ban compliance
item.description: The Australian internet regulator said it was investigating five of the biggest social media platforms for suspected breaches of its вҖӢnew under-16 ban, its strongest signal yet that companies вҖҢmay face enforcement action under a world-first regime.

item.title: Australia to ban surcharging on payment cards, deliver savings worth A$2.5 bln
item.description: SYDNEY, March 31 (Reuters) - Australia's central bank said on Tuesday that it would end surcharges on debit and credit card вҖӢpayments for consumers from October 1, while lowering вҖҢinterchange fees paid by businesses in steps estimated to save about A$2.5 billion a year.

item.title: Brazil's Natura announces stake purchase by Advent as founders exit board
item.description: Brazilian cosmetics maker Natura said on Monday вҖӢthat U.S. private equity firm вҖҢAdvent has signed a binding agreement to buy a stake in the company, while also вҖӢannouncing a board reshuffle that вҖӢincludes the departure of its founders.

item.title: Unilever-McCormick potential food unit deal includes $16В billion cash component, WSJ reports
item.description: Unilever вҖӢis in advanced talks to вҖҢcombine its food business with U.S. spice maker McCormick , in a deal вҖӢthat includes a cash вҖӢcomponent of around $16 billion, the Wall вҒ Street Journal reported on Monday, вҖӢciting people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Exclusive: Unilever imposes global hiring freeze, citing Middle East war effects, memo says
item.description: Dove soap maker Unilever has implemented a global вҖӢhiring freeze "at all levels" that will last at least three months, citing the effects вҖҢof the widening conflict in the Middle East, according to a memo seen by Reuters.

item.title: IMAX says CEO taking medical leave
item.description: IMAX said on Monday that CEO Richard Gelfond, вҖӢwho is being treated for вҖҢpneumonia, was taking a temporary medical leave.

item.title: GM idles Detroit EV plant, temporarily laying off 1,300
item.description: General Motors вҖӢis idling вҖҢa Detroit electric vehicle вҖӢplant until вҖӢApril 13, extending вҒ downtime that вҖӢbegan on вҖӢMarch 16, the company said вҖӢon Monday.

item.title: TRADING DAY Growth fears snowball
item.description: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit seven-month lows and bond yields fell on Monday as the Iran war entered its fifth week, with investors increasingly spooked by growth fears over inflation concerns even though oil prices rose further above $100 a barrel.

item.title: Fed's Williams says monetary policy 'well positioned' for 'unusual' circumstances
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams said on Monday that the current setting of monetary policy is in вҖӢa good place to deal with a range of challenges that very likely herald higher near-term inflation.

item.title: Verizon wins injunction blocking T-Mobile ad campaign promising big savings
item.description: A federal judge вҖҢon Monday granted Verizon Wireless a preliminary injunction blocking rival T-Mobile from running ads promising consumers more than $1,000 of annual savings if they switch cellphone carriers.

item.title: US business equipment borrowings rise more than 14% in February, ELFA says
item.description: U.S. companies borrowed 14.2% more to finance equipment purchases in February than a вҖӢyear earlier, fueled by a surge in вҖҢactivity among independent providers, the Equipment Leasing and Finance Association said on Monday.

item.title: Big Tech sports broadcast rights could imperil US local TV news, stations say
item.description: Major broadcast station owners on Monday said вҖӢU.S. regulators should вҖҢaddress the trend of Big Tech companies acquiring the вҖӢrights to broadcast вҖӢfootball, baseball and other вҒ sporting events, saying it вҖӢcould weaken local TV вҖӢnews.

item.title: JPMorgan must face Wells Fargo lawsuit over troubled $481 million real estate loan
item.description: A federal judge on Monday rejected JPMorgan вҖҢChase's bid to dismiss Wells Fargo's breach of contract lawsuit to recoup losses for investors in a defaulted $481 million commercial real estate loan.

item.title: Delaware judge accused of bias reassigns Musk cases
item.description: The chief judge on Delaware's corporate court said she will reassign three cases involving Elon Musk вҖӢto avoid unnecessary media attention after the billionaire entrepreneur complained that her activity on social media had shown bias вҖҢagainst him.

item.title: Exclusive: E*Trade in talks to lead SpaceX IPO share sale to small investors in US, sources say
item.description: Morgan Stanley's E*Trade is in talks вҖӢwith SpaceX to take the lead in selling the rocket maker's shares to everyday U.S. investors in its highly anticipated IPO later вҖҢthis year, giving it an edge over rival brokerages Robinhood Markets and SoFi, according to two people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Fed watching private credit sector for signs of trouble, Powell says
item.description: Federal Reserve вҖӢChair Jerome Powell said on Monday the U.S. вҖҢcentral bank is watching developments in the private credit sector for signs of trouble, but does not currently see issues there bringing вҖӢdown the financial system as a whole.

item.title: Italy data protection agency fines Intesa Sanpaolo $36 mln over data breach
item.description: Italy's data вҖӢprotection authority said on Monday it вҖҢhad fined the country's biggest bank Intesa Sanpaolo 31.8 million euro ($36.41 million) over a data breach вҖӢcase that involved some 3,500 customers вҖӢover two years.

item.title: Crypto fuels drone purchases in Russia and Iran, report says
item.description: Groups linked to Russia and Iran вҖӢare increasingly using cryptocurrency to finance the purchase of low-cost military drones and components, вҖҢaccording to a new report from blockchain analytics firm Chainalysis.

item.title: Components maker Elmet discloses revenue rise in US IPO filing
item.description: Precision-engineered components manufacturer Elmet Group on Monday revealed higher revenue for 2025 in its filing for an initial public вҖӢoffering in the United States, as it looks to вҖҢtap investor interest in defense-linked businesses.

item.title: US issues draft rules on private assets in 401k plans
item.description: The U.S. Department of Labor on Monday issued long-awaited proposed new rules intended to clarify how trustees can add alternative assets ranging from private вҖӢequity to cryptocurrencies to 401(k) retirement plans.
